Relationship to Skills: An activity requires many kinds of skills to successfully do; skills are performed in order to complete activities.
Relationship to Practices: An activity requires many kinds of practices to successfully do; practices are performed in order to complete activities.
Relationship to Projects: Many activities are involved in projects; a project requires many kinds of activities to successfully complete.
Relationship to Duties: Many activities are involved in performing duties; a duty requires many activities to successfully perform.
Relationship to Stakeholders: Activities involve many kinds of stakeholders; stakeholders participate in activities together.
Relationship to Project Milestones: Many activities are required to complete project milestones; project milestones involve many activities.
Relationship to Tools: An activity requires tools to use; tools enable the successful completion of activities.
Relationship to Deliverables: Activities enable people to produce deliverables; deliverables require many activities.
Relationship to Job Functions: Many activities are involved in job functions; job functions require people to perform activities.
Relationship to Specializations: An activity may be a part of many specializations; specializations involve completing activities
Relationship to Company Types: Many activities change based on the company type; different company types require different kinds of activities
Relationship to Methodologies: Activities use methodologies; methodologies involve people performing activities in certain ways
Relationship to Workshops: Activities are performed to complete workshops; workshops involve people performing activities together
